title: verbascum orientale subsp. orientale
intro: Verbascum orientale subsp. orientale, also known as Oriental Mullein, is a biennial plant that belongs to the figwort family. It is widely distributed across the Middle East, Central Asia, and parts of Eastern Europe, known for its tall spikes of yellow flowers and large, hairy leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, with its roots and leaves possessing medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Verbascum orientale subsp. orientale include its potential to soothe respiratory issues, reduce fever, and aid in wound healing, with herbal preparations often taking the form of teas, tinctures, and infusions.
